name: French Toast Cheddar Sandwiches
keywords: breads cheese eggs main dish sandwiches
recipeInstructions:
1. Set out a heavy skillet or cast iron griddle.
2. Beat the eggs slightly in a pie tin or shallow bowl and add the milk or cream and salt. Stir until well combined.
3. Spread the bread slices on a flat working surface.
4. Spread one side of four slices of bread lightly with prepared mustard. Top each with a slice of cheddar cheese.
5. Butter the remaining four slices of bread and top each cheese slice with bread, butter side down.
6. Heat the butter in the skillet or on the griddle until it begins to melt.
7. Carefully dip each sandwich into the egg mixture, coating both sides. Allow excess egg mixture to drain back into the bowl.
8. Dip only as many sandwiches as will lie flat in the skillet without overcrowding.
9. Cook over low heat until browned, about 2-3 minutes per side.
10. Turn and brown the other sides. Repeat for remaining sandwiches.
11. If necessary, add more butter to the skillet or griddle to prevent sticking.
12. Alternatively, place the sandwiches on a well-greased baking sheet and bake in an oven preheated to 450°F (230°C) for 8-10 minutes, flipping halfway through. Serve hot.
